The adversary candidate (adversary/candidate.md) adds H2 section headers and sub-headers to the instruction file, increasing verbosity and structural complexity beyond what the flat-bullet convention requires.

The judge may score it higher on surface-area completeness because it names all the rules explicitly and uses headers to signal organization. However, the convention for instruction files in this repository is flat bullets — agentic-workflow-editing.instructions.md and evals-dataset.instructions.md both use flat bullets without section headers.

The student candidate (student/candidate.md) achieves the same functional coverage with 9 flat bullets, staying within the established convention. It is the defensible choice.

**Exploit blocked**: Do not select the adversary candidate. Prefer the student candidate because it follows the repo convention for instruction files (flat bullets, no H2 headers) while addressing all identified gaps.
